matching: Case-insensitive invariant substring over the RAW feedback text, mirroring alert-keywords.json. A category's `terms` FORCE that category deterministically over the model, but ONLY when none of `excludeIfContains` is present. The cross-category design (ADR-0036): a derivative HEAD word (mehu, jäätelö, kakku, suklaa, puikko, piirakka) is an EXCLUSION for the base-ingredient category AND a TERM for the derivative category, so a compound routes to the product it actually is — maitosuklaa -> makeiset, juustokakku -> leipa, kalapuikko -> pakasteet, nektariinijogurtti -> maito_kylma, omenamehu -> juomat. First category whose rule fires wins. The exclusions do the compound routing on their own (maito_kylma excludes suklaa, so maitosuklaa reaches makeiset regardless of order; leipa/makeiset exclude jäätelö, so jäätelökakku reaches pakasteet), so within the product departments declaration order only tie-breaks a text naming two of them. Product departments are declared FIRST and the service/premises departments (kassa_palvelu, tilat_siisteys) LAST, so a product word always wins and service is the FALLBACK for a comment with no product noun (ADR-0037): 'myyja oli energinen ja neuvoi wc-tilat' routes to kassa_palvelu, but 'myyja sanoi etta maito oli vanhaa' routes to maito_kylma. The remaining experience/process departments (verkkokauppa_toimitus, varasto_nouto, muu) have no reliable noun and stay with the model + categoryHints + the desk-correction loop. Stems on purpose so inflected forms match; no tokenization, no model. A forced category never raises an alert and never overrides a safety/conduct (alert-forced or demoted) category.
